electron donor; specific rate only in Fig

growth_records.cite: Bae et al. Multi-omics analysis reveals the genetic basis for rapid CO2 utilization in the acetogenic bacterium Sporomusa sphaeroides KIAC. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00451-25. [PMC12456013]
growth_records.snippet: Under H2/CO2 conditions, KIAC reached an OD600 of 0.204 ± 0.010 with a maximum growth rate of 0.135 ± 0.003 h−1, producing 14.62 ± 0.52 mM acetate as the sole product. CO2 was completely depleted within 48 h.
growth_records.notes: Reported µmax=0.135±0.003 h⁻¹ → doubling = ln2/0.135 = 5.134 h. Autotrophic H2/CO2 acetogenesis; 14.62±0.52 mM acetate sole product (max OD600 0.204). CO2 fully consumed within 48 h. Medium is named DSM 311 with stated modifications; full salt/trace composition not itemized in paper → medium_composition_stated=false. Alt conditions (43 mM betaine, 50 mM formate, 25 mM methanol under N2) were used for omics but no growth rate reported for them.

up.method: KIAC 1.3-fold higher overall CO2 consumption; DSM2875 rate = KIAC/1.3 (Fig S1C)

growth_records.cite: Bae et al. Multi-omics analysis reveals the genetic basis for rapid CO2 utilization in the acetogenic bacterium Sporomusa sphaeroides KIAC. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00451-25. [PMC12456013]
growth_records.snippet: Compared to S. sphaeroides DSM 2875, KIAC exhibited a 1.6-fold higher growth rate and a 1.3-fold higher overall CO2 consumption rate.
growth_records.notes: KIAC µ=0.135 h⁻¹ is stated as 1.6-fold higher than DSM 2875 → DSM 2875 µ = 0.135/1.6 = 0.0844 h⁻¹ (calculated) → doubling = ln2/0.0844 = 8.21 h. CO2 consumption 1.3-fold lower than KIAC. Derived from a fold-change; low confidence.
